fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: Authenticate to AppDynamics using an API key. This will override username/password (basic) authenticationLeave blank for username/password authentication.

fields.label: Global Account Name
fields.description: The global account name, as shown in the Controller UI License page.

ui.placeholder: Paste in Analytics API Key here...

help.title: API Key
help.markdown:
You can generate and use an API key for each API access call into your Controller by generating an access token through the Administration UI. These API keys usually have a longer expiration. The account administrator can generate and distribute to parties/teams who need Controller access, but do not want to refresh such tokens frequently.

Create an API key in the Controller Administration UI under `Account > API Clients` (`<controller-url>/controller/#/location=ACCOUNT_ADMIN_API_CLIENTS`).

relationships.description: Basic auth credentials for the Controller (Metrics) API. Both are used together when root.basicAuth is true and no clientSecret is set (pkg/models/settings.go:49-51; pkg/appd/auth/auth_provider.go:56-64).

API Client (OAuth2 client-credentials) credentials for the Controller (Metrics) API. The backend builds client_id=clientName@clientDomain and posts client_secret to <url>/controller/api/oauth/access_token; all three are required together and take precedence over basic auth (pkg/appd/auth/auth_provider.go:66-83; pkg/appd/health_diagnostics.go:98-114).

Analytics (Events) API credentials, independent of the Controller auth. All three are required to enable Analytics; the backend sends analyticsAPIKey as X-Events-API-Key and globalAccountName as X-Events-API-AccountName against analyticsURL (pkg/appd/analytics/client.go:39-54; pkg/appd/health_diagnostics.go:70-84).

instructions.msg: The Controller (Metrics) API supports two authentication methods, and there is NO stored discriminator field: the backend picks the method at runtime. API Client auth is used when secureJsonData.clientSecret is set together with jsonData.clientName and jsonData.clientDomain (it builds an OAuth2 client_id of clientName@clientDomain); otherwise Basic auth is used with root.basicAuth=true, root.basicAuthUser and secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword. clientSecret overrides basic auth. root.url (the Controller URL, e.g. http://localhost:8086) is always required. Configure exactly one method.

Minimal API Client payload: {"url":"https://controller.example.com","jsonData":{"clientName":"<your-api-client-name>","clientDomain":"<your-account-name>"},"secureJsonData":{"clientSecret":"<your-api-client-secret>"}}. Minimal Basic auth payload: {"url":"https://controller.example.com","basicAuth":true,"basicAuthUser":"<your-username>","jsonData":{},"secureJsonData":{"basicAuthPassword":"<your-password>"}}. For API Client auth all three of clientName, clientDomain and clientSecret must be present; for Basic auth both the username and password must be present (pkg/appd/health_diagnostics.go:92-128).

Analytics (Events) is optional and configured independently of the Controller auth via jsonData.analyticsURL, jsonData.globalAccountName and secureJsonData.analyticsAPIKey. All three must be set for Analytics to be active; if any is missing the backend silently skips Analytics (no error) — it does not fall back to the Controller credentials (pkg/appd/health_diagnostics.go:70-84). analyticsURL is a separate Events API endpoint (NOT the Controller URL); the editor offers https://analytics.api.appdynamics.com, https://fra-ana-api.saas.appdynamics.com and https://syd-ana-api.saas.appdynamics.com but accepts any custom URL (e.g. an on-prem Events Service at http(s)://<controller-host>:9080/).

secureJsonData values (basicAuthPassword, clientSecret, analyticsAPIKey) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.clientSecret == true) instead of secureJsonData.

root.url is the AppDynamics Controller base URL and is read by the backend as settings.MetricsURL (pkg/models/settings.go:44); the backend path-joins Controller paths such as /controller/rest/applications and /controller/api/oauth/access_token onto it. Set jsonData.tlsSkipVerify=true only for self-signed Controllers (testing) — it is the single HTTP setting from Grafana's standard DataSourceHttpSettings that this backend honors (pkg/appd/client/client.go:39-41).

This datasource has no legacy storage shape and no auth-type discriminator, so do not infer the method from a stored field: a datasource with only basic-auth fields is Basic auth, and one with clientName/clientDomain/clientSecret is API Client auth. Grafana's DataSourceHttpSettings also renders With Credentials, access mode, custom headers, and TLS client-certificate fields (jsonData.tlsAuth, tlsAuthWithCACert, serverName, secureJsonData.tlsCACert/tlsClientCert/tlsClientKey), but this plugin's HTTP client ignores all of them except tlsSkipVerify and the proxy options (pkg/appd/client/client.go:29-44) — do not rely on them.
